title: वरलक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री वरलक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
पद्मासने पद्मकरे सर्वलोकैकपूजिते।
नारायणप्रिये देवि सुप्रीता भव सर्वदा॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥

सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके।
शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१॥

लक्ष्मीं क्षीरसमुद्रराजतनयां श्रीरङ्गधामेश्वरीं
दासीभूतसमस्तदेववनितां लोकैकदीपांकुराम्।
श्रीमन्मन्दकटाक्षलब्धविभवद्ब्रह्मेन्द्रगंगाधरां
त्वां त्रैलोक्यकुटुम्बिनीं सरसिजां वन्दे मुकुन्दप्रियाम्॥२॥

वरलक्ष्मि महालक्ष्मि विष्णुवक्षःस्थलस्थिते।
भक्तानामभयं कुरु देवि दारिद्र्यनाशिनि॥३॥

जयतु जयतु लक्ष्मीर्लक्षणैः शोभमाना
जयतु जयतु पद्मा पद्मसद्मासनस्था।
जयतु जयतु विद्या विष्णुवामांकसंस्था
जयतु जयतु सिद्धिः सिद्धविद्यासमृद्धा॥४॥

नमस्ते सर्वलोकानां जनन्यै पुण्यमूर्तये।
क्षीरसागरसम्भूते विष्णुपत्न्यै नमो नमः॥५॥

श्रीर्भूमिर्गौरी च सरस्वती च लक्ष्मीश्च वाणी श्रुतिसेव्यमाना।
पद्मासना पद्मदलायताक्षी श्रिये सदा मे भगवत्यनन्ता॥६॥

या सा पद्मासनस्था विपुलकटितटी पद्मपत्रायताक्षी
गम्भीरावर्तनाभिः स्तनभरनमिता शुभ्रवस्त्रोत्तरीया।
लक्ष्मीर्दिव्यैर्गजेन्द्रैर्मणिगणखचितैः स्नापिता हेमकुम्भैः
नित्यं सा पद्महस्ता मम वसतु गृहे सर्वमाङ्गल्ययुक्ता॥७॥

सिद्धलक्ष्मीर्मोक्षलक्ष्मीर्जयलक्ष्मीः सरस्वती।
श्रीलक्ष्मीर्वरलक्ष्मीश्च प्रसन्ना मम सर्वदा॥८॥

वरांकुशौ पाशमभीतिमुद्रां करैर्वहन्तीं कमलासनस्थाम्।
बालार्कवर्णां त्रिदशाभिवन्द्यां त्वां वन्दनारूपिणि वन्दयामि॥९॥

सर्वज्ञे सर्ववरदे सर्वदुष्टभयंकरि।
सर्वदुःखहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१०॥

सिद्धिबुद्धिप्रदे देवि भुक्तिमुक्तिप्रदायिनि।
मन्त्रमूर्ते सदा देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥११॥

आद्यन्तरहिते देवि आद्यशक्तिमहेश्वरि।
योगजे योगसम्भूते महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१२॥

स्थूलसूक्ष्ममहारौद्रे महाशक्तिमहोदरे।
महापापहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१३॥

पद्मासनस्थिते देवि परब्रह्मस्वरूपिणि।
परमेशि जगन्मातर्महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१४॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
वरलक्ष्मीमहास्तोत्रं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
श्रावणे शुक्रवारे च विशेषाद्व्रतपूर्वकम्॥
सर्वसम्पत्समृद्धिं स लभते नात्र संशयः।
पुत्रपौत्रधनं लक्ष्मीं लभते सुखमक्षयम्॥

॥ इति श्री वरलक्ष्मी स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Varalakshmi Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Padmasane Padmakare Sarva-lokaika-pujite.
Narayana-priye Devi Suprita Bhava Sarvada.

|| Stotram ||

Sarva-mangala-mangalye Shive Sarvartha-sadhike.
Sharanye Tryambake Gauri Narayani Namo'stu Te.||1||

Lakshmim Kshira-samudra-raja-tanayam Shrirangha-dhameshvarim
Dasi-bhuta-samasta-deva-vanitam Lokaika-dipankuram.
Shriman-manda-kataksha-labdha-vibhavad-Brahm-endra-Gangadharam
Tvam Trailokya-kutumbinim Sarasijam Vande Mukunda-priyam.||2||

Varalakshmi Mahalakshmi Vishnu-vakshah-sthala-sthite.
Bhaktanam-abhayam Kuru Devi Daridrya-nashini.||3||

Jayatu Jayatu Lakshmih Lakshanairh Shobhamana
Jayatu Jayatu Padma Padma-sadma-asanastha.
Jayatu Jayatu Vidya Vishnu-vamanka-samstha
Jayatu Jayatu Siddhih Siddha-vidya-samriddha.||4||

Namaste Sarva-lokanam Jananyai Punya-murtaye.
Kshira-sagara-sambhute Vishnu-patnyai Namo Namah.||5||

Shrir-Bhumir-Gauri cha Sarasvati cha Lakshmish-cha Vani Shruti-sevyamana.
Padmasana Padma-dalayatakshi Shriye Sada Me Bhagavaty-ananta.||6||

Ya Sa Padmasana-stha Vipula-kati-tati Padma-patrayatakshi
Gambhira-avarta-nabhih Stana-bhara-namita Shubhra-vastrottariya.
Lakshmih Divyaih Gajendrair-mani-gana-khachitaih Snapita Hema-kumbhaih
Nityam Sa Padma-hasta Mama Vasatu Grihe Sarva-mangalya-yukta.||7||

Siddha-Lakshmih Moksha-Lakshmih Jaya-Lakshmih Sarasvati.
Shri-Lakshmih Vara-Lakshmish-cha Prasanna Mama Sarvada.||8||

Varan-kushau Pasham-abhiti-mudram Karair-vahantim Kamalasana-stham.
Balarka-varnam Tridasha-abhivandyam Tvam Vandana-rupini Vandayami.||9||

Sarvajne Sarva-varade Sarva-dushta-bhayankari.
Sarva-duhkha-hare Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||10||

Siddhi-buddhi-prade Devi Bhukti-mukti-pradayini.
Mantra-murte Sada Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||11||

Adyanta-rahite Devi Adya-shakti-maheshvari.
Yoga-je Yoga-sambhute Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||12||

Sthula-sukshma-maharaudre Maha-shakti-mahodare.
Maha-papa-hare Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||13||

Padmasana-sthite Devi Parabrahma-svarupini.
Parameshi Jagan-matar-Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||14||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Varalakshmi-maha-stotram Yah Pathed-bhakti-samyutah.
Shravane Shukravare cha Visheshad-vrata-purvakam.
Sarva-sampat-samriddhim Sa Labhate Natra Samshayah.
Putra-pautra-dhanam Lakshmim Labhate Sukham-akshayam.

|| Iti Shri Varalakshmi Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये — सब मंगलों में मंगल रूप, शिवे — कल्याणकारी, सर्वार्थसाधिके — सब पुरुषार्थों को सिद्ध करने वाली, शरण देने वाली, त्रिनेत्रधारी, गौरी, नारायणी को नमस्कार।

क्षीर सागर राजा की पुत्री, श्रीरंग की स्वामिनी, जिसके सौम्य कटाक्ष से ब्रह्मा-इन्द्र-शंकर को ऐश्वर्य मिला, ऐसी तीनों लोकों की गृहस्वामिनी, कमलासन, मुकुन्दप्रिया लक्ष्मी को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ।

हे वरलक्ष्मी! हे महालक्ष्मी! विष्णु के वक्षःस्थल पर विराजमान, भक्तों को अभय दो, दारिद्र्य का नाश करो।

लक्ष्मी जय हो! पद्मा जय हो! विद्या जय हो! सिद्धि जय हो!

हे सर्वज्ञे! सर्ववरदे! सब दुष्टों को भय देने वाली, सब दुःख हरने वाली, सिद्धि-बुद्धि देने वाली, भुक्ति-मुक्ति दायिनी, महापाप हरने वाली, परब्रह्म स्वरूपिणी, जगन्माता महालक्ष्मी को नमस्कार।

श्रावण शुक्रवार को व्रतपूर्वक इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से सर्वसम्पत्ति, पुत्र-पौत्र, धन-लक्ष्मी और अक्षय सुख प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Primary stotra for Varalakshmi Vrat observed on Fridays in Shravana month
Invokes all-round prosperity, wealth, and material abundance
Invokes the blessing of progeny, grandchildren, and family happiness
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty and ensures permanent residence of lakshmi
Invokes Siddha, Moksha, Jaya, and Vara forms of Lakshmi together
Especially powerful when recited with vrat on Shravana Shukravara
description: Varalakshmi Stotram (वरलक्ष्मी स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi. This revered text originates from Skanda Purana (Varalakshmi Vrat Katha). Regular recitation is believed to primary stotra for Varalakshmi Vrat observed on Fridays in Shravana month, grant all-round prosperity, wealth, and material abundance, and invoke Siddha, Moksha, Jaya, and Vara forms of Lakshmi together. It is especially recommended on Friday (Shukravar) and during Varalakshmi Vrata and Diwali for maximum spiritual benefit. With 14 verses and a reading time of about 10 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Varalakshmi Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Varalakshmi Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn for Varalakshmi Vrat for wealth, progeny, and prosperity.
relatedSearches: Varalakshmi Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit Telugu Tamil
Varalakshmi puja vidhi step by step
Eight Lakshmis invoked in Varalakshmi Vrata
Varalakshmi Stotram benefits for married women

seoFaqs.question: What does the name 'Varalakshmi' literally mean?
seoFaqs.answer: Varalakshmi combines vara ('boon, blessing') with Lakshmi, yielding 'the boon-bestowing Lakshmi.' She is the manifestation of Mahalakshmi who specifically grants requested boons (varadayini), and her vrata is performed with sankalpas (vows) for particular wishes — typically the husband's longevity and welfare, family prosperity, children's education, financial stability and protection from misfortune. The Rudraksha-Ratna and Vedadhara descriptions emphasise that worshipping Varalakshmi on her appointed day is considered equivalent to simultaneously worshipping all eight Ashtalakshmis. (Sources: Rudraksha-Ratna; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Varalakshmi Vrata observed?
seoFaqs.answer: Varalakshmi Vrata falls on the Friday immediately preceding Shravana Purnima — i.e. the second Friday of the bright fortnight (Shukla paksha) of the Shravana month in the lunar calendar. In the Gregorian calendar this typically falls in late July or early August. The day is observed predominantly across Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka and parts of Maharashtra. Married women (sumangalis) wake before sunrise, complete a ritual bath, decorate a kalasha as the goddess, perform the puja, recite the stotram and conclude with sumangali tambulam (gift exchange between married women). (Sources: thedailyjagran; Rudraksha-Ratna; Seema.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the legend of Charumati associated with this vrata?
seoFaqs.answer: The Skanda Purana narrative recounts that Goddess Varalakshmi appeared in the dream of Charumati, a virtuous Brahmin woman of Kundinapura, and instructed her: 'On the Friday that comes before Shravana Purnima, worship me, and I shall bless you with all your desired boons.' Charumati performed the prescribed puja with her neighbours; their households were blessed with wealth, ornaments and progeny, and the vrata spread from there. The story is recited (katha-shravana) as part of the puja, between the kalasha-sthapana and the aarti. (Sources: Vedadhara; thedailyjagran.)

seoFaqs.question: What stotras are recited alongside the Varalakshmi Stotram on vrata day?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard recitation order in most South Indian manuals begins with Ganesha pranama, then Lakshmi dhyana, followed by: Sri Suktam (Rig Veda khila), Lakshmi Ashtottara Shatanamavali (108 names), Mahalakshmi Ashtakam (from the Padma Purana), Kanakadhara Stotram (Adi Shankara), Ashtalakshmi Stotram, and finally the Varalakshmi Stotram itself. After the stotras, the puja concludes with mangalarati and distribution of prasada — typically pongal, kozhukattai or sundal, payasam, and turmeric-coated tambulam. (Sources: ePoojaStore; Desifavors; Rudraksha-Ratna.)

seoFaqs.question: Which eight forces or 'Ashtalakshmis' are invoked through Varalakshmi worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Varalakshmi worship is held to invoke eight aspects simultaneously: Sri (wealth), Bhu (earth and stability), Saraswati (learning and speech), Preeti (love and affection), Keerti (fame and reputation), Shanti (peace), Tushti (contentment), and Pushti (nourishment and strength). This eight-fold framing is slightly different from the standard Ashtalakshmi list (Adi, Dhanya, etc.) and reflects the Varalakshmi-vrata's origin as a household-prosperity rite rather than a temple ashtaka. Each of the eight is invoked through a specific kumkuma-archana name during the puja sequence. (Sources: thedailyjagran; Seema.)

seoFaqs.question: Can unmarried women, widows or men perform the Varalakshmi Vrata?
seoFaqs.answer: The vrata is traditionally framed for sumangalis (women whose husbands are living), and the most prominent symbolic items — yellow thread (kankanam), bangles, mangalsutra-decoration of the kalasha — express that emphasis. However, contemporary practice in many regions also welcomes unmarried women seeking compatible marriages, widows seeking general welfare, and male householders performing the puja on behalf of their families. Vedadhara and Pujalane note that the vrata is fundamentally a Lakshmi-aradhana and its scope of grace is therefore not limited; the only firm rule is sincerity of sankalpa and ritual cleanliness. (Sources: Pujalane; Vedadhara; Seema.)
